last_item: Mode-F (Alerts): block creating a NEW enabled notification channel with no credentials. Surfaced by the iter-24 final Mode-S convergence panel (4 of 5 views converged; the Alerts/Migrate agent floated two Alerts candidates). Candidate 1 (Switch lacks loading/optimistic-revert) self-rejected on inspection: the shadcn/Radix Switch is fully controlled by checked={rule.enabled} bound to server data, so a failed toggleRule never moves the thumb — no misleading state, false premise. Candidate 2 verified REAL against the backend.
last_result: SHIPPED — 4 SHIP, zero blockers (restraint critic included). The trap: handleSaveAlertChannel (internal/server/handlers_alerts.go) validates ONLY the channel kind, never credential presence, so an enabled Pushover/Webhook channel saved with blank token/user/URL succeeds; the ChannelCard then shows green 'Configured' (configured = Boolean(config?.enabled)) and the existing rulesWontFire guard misses it (it only checks c.enabled, not whether creds exist) — a SILENT dead alert pipeline on the one feature whose job is to warn you. Fix in ChannelModal: requiredMissing (pushover: token|user blank; webhook: url blank) + blockEmptyNew = !config && enabled && requiredMissing; Save button disabled={busy||blockEmptyNew} + explanatory title. Scoped to NEW channels (!config) on purpose: existing channels' secret fields (PushoverToken, WebhookURL) come back blank because maskAlertChannels strips them (write-only), so a blank token on edit means 'keep the stored secret' — gating that would break the edit flow. A disabled draft channel can still be saved with blanks. ~3 lines, no new component/visible copy. Restraint critic affirmed: FIRST and ONLY guard on a currently-unguarded silent-failure state, NOT an Nth redundant warning; 'Send test' is optional/skippable so not sufficient mitigation; disabling a submit on missing required fields is the most baseline form affordance. Test added (asserts a new enabled pushover channel can't be saved blank, then saves once token+user filled). Gates: typecheck ✓, 146 tests ✓ (145 + 1 new), build ✓ (dist regenerated + staged), go build ✓ (exit 0, outside sandbox). stable_streak RESETS 2 -> 0 (real improvement shipped). NOTE: the backend still accepts a blank-credential enabled channel — this is a client-side guard; the durable fix is handler-side credential validation (out of this frontend-only loop's scope; filed in backlog).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn -- rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres -- minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
